prompt: A multi-region e-commerce system with Cosmos DB for global product catalog, Azure Front Door for traffic routing, Redis Cache for sessions, and Event Grid for order processing

services.name: Azure Front Door
services.description: Global HTTP entry point and multi-region traffic routing for e-commerce front-end

services.name: App Service
services.description: Multi-region e-commerce web application and APIs handling user sessions and orders

services.name: Azure Cache for Redis
services.description: Distributed cache for user sessions, shopping carts, and frequently accessed data

services.name: Azure Functions
services.description: Serverless background processing for order events and downstream workflows

services.name: Azure Cosmos DB
services.description: Globally distributed product catalog and order data store

services.name: Storage Account
services.description: Static content, order documents, and media assets backing the storefront

services.name: Azure Event Grid
services.description: Event routing backbone for order placed, updated, and fulfillment events

services.name: Microsoft Entra ID
services.description: Customer and admin authentication and authorization for the e-commerce site

services.name: Azure Monitor
services.description: Central monitoring service collecting metrics and logs from the web app

services.name: Log Analytics
services.description: Log analytics workspace for querying and analyzing application telemetry

connections.label: Route customer HTTP(S) requests to closest healthy web app region
Serve static assets such as images and scripts for product pages
Read and write user session state and shopping cart contents
Query global product catalog and persist order records
Publish OrderPlaced and OrderUpdated events for asynchronous processing
Trigger serverless order workflows when new order events arrive
Update order status, inventory, and audit records after processing
Store order-related documents such as invoices or packing slips
Emit data change notifications for order and catalog updates
Redirect users for sign-in and validate tokens for secured APIs
Send application metrics, traces, and request logs
Ingest consolidated telemetry for querying and alerting

workflow.description: Customer navigates to the e-commerce site, and Azure Front Door routes the request to the nearest healthy App Service instance.
App Service delegates authentication to Microsoft Entra ID and establishes a user session, optionally using Azure Cache for Redis for session state.
For browsing, App Service retrieves product catalog data from Azure Cosmos DB and static assets from the Storage Account, returning composed product pages.
As users add items to the cart, App Service updates shopping cart state in Azure Cache for Redis and records interim data in Azure Cosmos DB if needed.
When checkout is completed, App Service writes the order to Azure Cosmos DB and immediately publishes an OrderPlaced event to Azure Event Grid.
Azure Event Grid invokes Azure Functions to handle order processing tasks such as inventory checks, payment confirmation, and fulfillment orchestration.
Azure Functions updates the order status and inventory documents in Azure Cosmos DB and can store related artifacts such as invoices in the Storage Account.
Changes to orders or catalog entries in Azure Cosmos DB emit events through Azure Event Grid, enabling further downstream integrations if required.
Throughout the flow, App Service sends metrics and logs to Azure Monitor, which aggregates telemetry in Log Analytics for troubleshooting and optimization.

Issue and validate OAuth/OIDC tokens for authenticated requests
Provide secrets and certificates at startup and during key rotation
Read/write user sessions and cart state for low-latency access

Publish OrderPlaced event after successful checkout submission
Trigger order-processing function per OrderPlaced event subscription
Create order record and update inventory/reservation state

Centralize collected telemetry into workspace for querying and alerts
Redirect users to sign-in and complete authorization code flow

workflow.description: Customer hits the e-commerce URL; Azure Front Door routes the request to the nearest healthy region hosting the app.
User signs in; the application uses Microsoft Entra ID for authentication and token validation on protected endpoints.
The application retrieves required secrets (e.g., database keys/connection strings) from Key Vault to access downstream services securely.
Browsing and cart actions use Redis for session/cart state and Cosmos DB for global product catalog lookups.
On checkout, the application records checkout metadata and publishes an OrderPlaced event for asynchronous processing.
Event Grid delivers the OrderPlaced event to an Azure Functions handler to process the order without blocking the user request.
The Functions handler writes the final order state to Cosmos DB and optionally invalidates Redis session/cart cache entries.
Application telemetry is sent to Azure Monitor and stored in Log Analytics for investigation, dashboards, and alerting.

Validate user sign-in and acquire access tokens for checkout sessions
Retrieve API keys and database credentials at runtime
Read and update cart/session state for active shoppers

workflow.description: Customer traffic enters globally and is sent to the best available regional app instance.
Application authenticates the user and retrieves required secrets before serving commerce actions.
Product pages and carts are served using global catalog data and low-latency session cache.
On checkout, order data is stored and an OrderPlaced event is published for downstream processing.
Event Grid pushes order events to Functions, which execute fulfillment and status logic.
Functions write updated order/inventory state and publish lifecycle events for application consumption.
Application receives lifecycle notifications and exposes updated order state to customers.
Primary application telemetry is centralized for alerting and log analytics.

workflow.description: Azure Front Door evaluates health and directs shoppers to the primary regional storefront, with the secondary region available for failover.
The selected regional App Service loads the customer's session and shopping cart from the co-located Redis cache.
The regional storefront retrieves product information from the globally distributed Cosmos DB catalog during browsing and checkout.
After checkout completes, the storefront writes order data and publishes an order-created event to Event Grid.
Event Grid delivers each order event to Azure Functions for asynchronous order processing.
Azure Functions updates order status and related metadata in Cosmos DB as processing advances.
The primary application sends runtime telemetry to Azure Monitor for centralized collection.
Azure Monitor forwards collected logs and metrics into Log Analytics for troubleshooting and operational analysis.

workflow.description: A shopper enters the site through the global edge and is routed to the healthiest regional application instance.
The application authenticates the user with Microsoft Entra ID and retrieves runtime secrets from Key Vault.
The application restores the customer session from Redis Cache and reads the latest product catalog from Cosmos DB.
When the customer places an order, the application publishes an order event to Event Grid.
Event Grid triggers Azure Functions to process the order, update order records, and adjust inventory in Cosmos DB.
The function app updates cached session and cart data in Redis Cache and resolves any required downstream secrets from Key Vault.
The application emits telemetry to Azure Monitor, which forwards logs to Log Analytics for centralized observability.

workflow.description: User accesses the e-commerce site. Azure Front Door routes the request to the nearest App Service instance.
App Service authenticates the user via Microsoft Entra ID and retrieves secrets from Key Vault.
App Service reads product catalog from Cosmos DB, with Redis Cache as a session store and cache for frequently accessed data.
When a customer places an order, App Service publishes an order event to Event Grid.
Event Grid triggers an Azure Function to process the order asynchronously.
The Function updates order status in Cosmos DB and invalidates relevant cache entries in Redis.
All services emit telemetry to Azure Monitor, which forwards logs to Log Analytics for analysis.
